title: syntax error in the SMT-LIBv2 output generated by write_smt2
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.25+86 (git sha1 541fdffff, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Verilog code:
```verilog
module foo(b, a0, a1, o);

  input b;
  input signed [5:0] a0;
  input signed [3:0] a1;

  output [4:0] o;

  assign o = b?a0:(|a1);

endmodule
```

Yosys script:
```
read_verilog -formal {verilog_filepath}; hierarchy -check -top foo
proc; opt; memory -nordff -nomap; opt -fast
dffunmap; write_smt2 -wires
```

Yosys was invoked by this command:
```
yosys -qq -p "{script above}"
```

### Expected Behavior

The given circuit is encoded into smt2 format, and written to stdout.

### Actual Behavior

When I fed the smt2 output to Z3 solver, it yielded several errors:
```
(error "line 20 column 89: Sorts (_ BitVec 6) and (_ BitVec 4) are incompatible")
(error "line 21 column 124: unknown constant foo#3 (foo_s) ")
(error "line 24 column 85: unknown constant foo#4 (foo_s) ")
```

And I found an ill-formed formula there:
```
(distinct (concat #b00 (|foo#1| state)) #b0000))
```
It seems that this formula is related to the `(|a1)` expression in the source code. But a width mismatch problem occurred.

The full output of `write_smt2` is listed below.
```
; SMT-LIBv2 description generated by Yosys 0.25+86 (git sha1 541fdffff, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)
; yosys-smt2-module foo
(declare-sort |foo_s| 0)
(declare-fun |foo_is| (|foo_s|) Bool)
(declare-fun |foo#0| (|foo_s|) (_ BitVec 6)) ; \a0
; yosys-smt2-input a0 6
; yosys-smt2-wire a0 6
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\a0"], "smtname": "a0", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 6}
(define-fun |foo_n a0| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 6) (|foo#0| state))
(declare-fun |foo#1| (|foo_s|) (_ BitVec 4)) ; \a1
; yosys-smt2-input a1 4
; yosys-smt2-wire a1 4
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\a1"], "smtname": "a1", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 4}
(define-fun |foo_n a1| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|foo#1| state))
(declare-fun |foo#2| (|foo_s|) Bool) ; \b
; yosys-smt2-input b 1
; yosys-smt2-wire b 1
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\b"], "smtname": "b", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 1}
(define-fun |foo_n b| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool (|foo#2| state))
■■■ Here is the problematic line ■■■
(define-fun |foo#3| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool (distinct (concat #b00 (|foo#1| state)) #b0000)) ; $reduce_or$foo/foo.v:9$1_Y
(define-fun |foo#4| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 6) (ite (|foo#2| state) (|foo#0| state) (concat #b00000 (ite (|foo#3| state) #b1 #b0)))) ; { $ternary$foo/foo.v:9$2_Y [5] \o }
; yosys-smt2-output o 5
; yosys-smt2-wire o 5
(define-fun |foo_n o| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 5) ((_ extract 4 0) (|foo#4| state)))
(define-fun |foo_a| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_u| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_i| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_h| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_t| ((state |foo_s|) (next_state |foo_s|)) Bool true) ; end of module foo
; yosys-smt2-topmod foo
; end of yosys output
```

p.s.
I've tried the same test case with **the current git master** (f7c1e4aadfcd168274f65034c4e66ed96271d8ec) of Yosys. The problem still exists.

p.p.s.
Thanks for reading. Hope I've made no mistakes with the usage of yosys.
code_blocks:
read_verilog -formal {verilog_filepath}; hierarchy -check -top foo
proc; opt; memory -nordff -nomap; opt -fast
dffunmap; write_smt2 -wires

; SMT-LIBv2 description generated by Yosys 0.25+86 (git sha1 541fdffff, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)
; yosys-smt2-module foo
(declare-sort |foo_s| 0)
(declare-fun |foo_is| (|foo_s|) Bool)
(declare-fun |foo#0| (|foo_s|) (_ BitVec 6)) ; \a0
; yosys-smt2-input a0 6
; yosys-smt2-wire a0 6
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\a0"], "smtname": "a0", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 6}
(define-fun |foo_n a0| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 6) (|foo#0| state))
(declare-fun |foo#1| (|foo_s|) (_ BitVec 4)) ; \a1
; yosys-smt2-input a1 4
; yosys-smt2-wire a1 4
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\a1"], "smtname": "a1", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 4}
(define-fun |foo_n a1| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|foo#1| state))
(declare-fun |foo#2| (|foo_s|) Bool) ; \b
; yosys-smt2-input b 1
; yosys-smt2-wire b 1
; yosys-smt2-witness {"offset": 0, "path": ["\\b"], "smtname": "b", "smtoffset": 0, "type": "input", "width": 1}
(define-fun |foo_n b| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool (|foo#2| state))
■■■ Here is the problematic line ■■■
(define-fun |foo#3| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool (distinct (concat #b00 (|foo#1| state)) #b0000)) ; $reduce_or$foo/foo.v:9$1_Y
(define-fun |foo#4| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 6) (ite (|foo#2| state) (|foo#0| state) (concat #b00000 (ite (|foo#3| state) #b1 #b0)))) ; { $ternary$foo/foo.v:9$2_Y [5] \o }
; yosys-smt2-output o 5
; yosys-smt2-wire o 5
(define-fun |foo_n o| ((state |foo_s|)) (_ BitVec 5) ((_ extract 4 0) (|foo#4| state)))
(define-fun |foo_a| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_u| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_i| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_h| ((state |foo_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |foo_t| ((state |foo_s|) (next_state |foo_s|)) Bool true) ; end of module foo
; yosys-smt2-topmod foo
; end of yosys output
